calendar.matters: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-17-2026 Continued To Jul-20-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
TENTATIVE RULING: Hearing. Appearance required.

The procedural issues have not been cleared since Jan, 2026 hearing:
(1) The original Will has not been submitted, and the petition has not been updated to request admission of a lost will. Although the petitioner filed a declaration regarding the original will on 2/26/2026, it does not appear to support the petition. To proceed, the petitioner must submit the original Will if seeking its admission. If the petitioner intends to admit a lost will, an amended petition with the required details will likely be necessary. The Court will not resolve the issue for the petitioner
(2) According to the supplement, Scott H. appointed Joseph B. and Cassandra S. as executors. The Court does not have clear authority to appoint the petitioner as executor when the named executor's written declination to act is not on file. Also, allegations of other persons that the executor contradicts the relief of this petition. Scott H's written declination to act must be filed, along with his nomination of other executors.
(3) The petition does not name the trustee of the residual beneficiary trust, and proper notice to the trustee is required. The notice on file lists several beneficiaries, but addresses for Robert B., Cassandra S., and Marisa Leonis are missing.
(4) The petition must clarify how the petitioner intends to address the conflict of interest resulting from their role as a creditor.
(5) A full bond will be required.
(6) No notices have been provided to Hodge, Joseph B., Cassandra B., or their counsel, Attorney Sun.
(7) Petition #8 does not identify the heirs at law. While three relatives are listed as trust beneficiaries, the petition does not confirm they are the only heirs.
(8) Petition #3 alleges that the estimated value of the property is less than $208,850. Since the above issues have not been resolved, the Court may infer that this probate proceeding is not necessary.

PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
EXAMINER: AEYOUNG KIM
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Melbostad for Thomas Buckner, petitioner (via zoom).
Myron Gluksmen for Jessie Gonzalez, creditor (via zoom).

Discusssion was held regarding the appointment made by Scott Hodges the original executor, that he designated Cassandra Leonis as executor, and that the original will was turned over to Cassandra.

Mr. Melbostad stated that his client had to advance his own funds for the estate and deal with the creditors' claim, that counsel Byron Sun has not lodged the will with the Courts, and that he would like to pursue the estate in probate due to the creditor's claims.

The Court stated that the case is not ripe to admit into probate, and that the original will has not been lodged with the Courts.

The matter is continued to July 20, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.
